mission.title: wasmcloud: [FEATURE] Washboard should display assets that are managed by wadm
mission.description: [FEATURE] Washboard should display assets that are managed by wadm. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current wasmcloud deployment
steps.description:
Verify your wasmcloud version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
helm list -n wasmcloud 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working wasmcloud installation.

steps.title: Review wasmcloud configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant wasmcloud configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
kubectl get configmap -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=wasmcloud
```
As we develop wadm, I realized that there's not an easy way to determine if a resource is managed by wadm or not.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [FEATURE] Washboard should display assets that are managed…
steps.description:
## Feature or Problem

Some of the resources are managed by wadm and it is not easy to know which one is manually created and which one is by wadm.
Obviously the feature flag part is part of the WIP only.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
kubectl get events -n wasmcloud --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[FEATURE] Washboard should display assets that are managed…" is functioning correctly.
